title: Hydropark Widget — Base Contract (canonical form)
description: The common envelope every widget instance conforms to (SPEC 9.2). Per-widget schemas (chat, timer_stack, editable_list, …) `allOf`-extend this file: they pin `type` to a const, tighten `props`, add per-type `required`, and reference the shared `$defs` here (slot type language, event decls, style variants). This schema validates the CANONICAL widget object; a skill manifest may author the SHORTHAND projection (inline props, omitted region/priority/emits/min_widget_version), which the app normalizes to this canonical form at load before validating (SPEC 9.2 'One canonical schema; the manifest uses shorthand'). Unknown/too-new `type` values are NOT rejected here — they degrade to a graceful placeholder at render (SPEC 9.8); see $defs/widgetTypeName.

title.description: Panel heading. Localizable (see $defs/localizableString). Also the default accessible name when a11y.label is absent.

region.description: Layout dock region. Optional in the manifest; normalization fills the widget-type's default region (SPEC 9.5).

priority.description: Placement order within a region (higher first). Panels from all active skills are placed by region then priority, then deduplicated by type+id+binding (SPEC 8.3.3 / 9.5).

min_widget_version.description: Minimum widget-LIBRARY version this instance needs. If it exceeds the installed library version (or `type` is unknown), the app renders a placeholder, never a crash/blank (SPEC 9.8).

props.description: Typed, per-type configuration. The base leaves this OPEN; each per-widget schema constrains it (own field set + additionalProperties:false). In shorthand the manifest may inline these props at the top level; normalization folds them under `props` (SPEC 9.2).

binds_state.description: Two-way binding to a shared-state slot (SPEC 8.3.4). The widget renders the slot's live value and, IFF its owning skill is the slot's writer-of-record, its edits write back; otherwise it binds READ-ONLY (renders live, edit affordances disabled, tooltip naming the writer — SPEC 8.3.4 / 9.3 #1). The effective read/write mode is RESOLVED at merge time; it is not authored here.

reads_state.description: Additional slots this widget observes READ-ONLY for display beyond its primary `binds_state` (e.g. a key_value_panel showing macros for the `ingredients` slot it does not own). Routing is keyed on slot NAME, so an update to any listed slot re-renders this widget (SPEC 9.3 'Tool ↔ slot ↔ widget routing').

binds_tool.description: User actions on this widget invoke this tool deterministically — the UI-first path, no model round-trip (SPEC 9.3 #2 / 8.4). May be a namespaced ref (e.g. `cooking.convert_units`) after collision resolution (SPEC 8.3.3).

emits.description: Events this widget posts to the agent's event bus (SPEC 9.3 #4). Each carries a `to_chat` flag — a per-widget-type default, overridable per event. Optional in the manifest; normalization fills the widget-type's default event set. Certification rejects duplicate event names.

states.description: Optional COPY overrides for the mandatory loading/empty/error states. The widget TYPE ships sensible defaults so skills never have to (SPEC 9.2); a skill may only override the localizable strings, never suppress a state.

widgetId.description: Unique within the agent (cross-widget uniqueness is enforced at merge, not by this schema). Snake_case.

widgetTypeName.description: Snake_case widget-type identifier. Deliberately NOT a closed enum: a manifest built against a newer library may reference a type an older app has never heard of, and that MUST render a placeholder (SPEC 9.8), not fail validation. The known v1 registry is $defs/knownWidgetTypeV1 — used by the renderer's 'is this type installed?' check and by certification, not to gate envelope validity.

knownWidgetTypeV1.description: The v1 first-party widget library (SPEC 9.4). Non-normative for envelope validation; the renderer uses it to decide render-vs-placeholder, and per-widget schemas pin `type` to one of these. `chart`/`sparkline`/`map` are deferred to Phase 2 and intentionally absent.

region.description: SPEC 9.5. Below the minimum window width, `side` panels move to a bottom drawer (layout engine, P1-06.5).

widgetVersion.description: MAJOR.MINOR[.PATCH]. The manifest example uses the two-part form ("1.0").

slotName.description: Name of a shared-state slot (SPEC 8.3.4). Routing between tools and widgets is keyed on this name.

toolRefName.description: A fixed-catalog tool ref (SPEC 8.1/8.5). The optional `skill.tool` dotted form is the namespaced variant produced by collision resolution (SPEC 8.3.3).

localizableString.description: Display text. May be a literal (default-locale/dev) or an `@`-prefixed key resolved against the skill's localized string table (SPEC 8.7). Certification verifies key completeness for every declared locale.

eventDecl.description: An emitted event, in canonical (object) or shorthand (bare name string) form. Shorthand uses the widget-type's default `to_chat` for that event; the object form overrides it (SPEC 9.2 / 9.3 #4).

to_chat.description: true posts a system line into the transcript when the event fires ('⏱ Pasta timer finished'). Posting appends to the transcript (and, if time-critical, fires the OS notification, SPEC 9.7) — it NEVER auto-runs inference (SPEC 9.3 #4).

stateCopyOverrides.description: Per-state copy overrides. Presence of a key overrides only that state's strings; absence uses the widget-type default. A skill cannot disable a state.

a11yMeta.description: Accessible-name/description overrides the skill supplies. The MECHANICAL accessibility contract — keyboard operability, ARIA roles, focus order, per-theme WCAG-AA contrast, and no-hue-only meaning (WCAG 1.4.1) — is guaranteed by the first-party widget-library implementation and enforced by the X-A11Y gate; it is NOT the skill's to author or waive.

live_region.description: aria-live politeness for content that updates out-of-band (e.g. a finished timer). Default is the widget-type's own choice.

styleVariants.description: The ENTIRE skill-facing styling surface (P1-03.8). A skill selects from this closed vocabulary of semantic variants only; each maps first-party to design tokens (color/spacing/type/radius). Skills ship NO CSS, NO raw token references, NO inline styles (SPEC 9.1/9.10). A certification lint rejects any non-token styling in the widget library itself and any attempt to smuggle raw style through the manifest (P1-20.1). NOTE: `tone` must never be the SOLE carrier of meaning — the widget always pairs it with text/icon (WCAG 1.4.1); see $defs/designTokenNamespace for the underlying vocabulary the lint enforces.

tone.description: Semantic color role. Maps per-theme to AA-safe token pairs; never hue-only (paired with label/icon).

designTokenNamespace.description: NON-NORMATIVE for the manifest (skills never write these). This is the closed design-token vocabulary the widget-library IMPLEMENTATION draws from and the target of the non-token-styling lint (P1-03.8/P1-20.1): every color/spacing/type/radius value in widget CSS must be a `var(--token)` from these namespaces — no literal hex/px/rem/keyword styling. Enumerated so the lint and the token system (P1-01.3) share one definition.

slotType.description: The closed shared-state type language (SPEC 8.3.4), NORMALIZED object form. A slot's authored `schema` string (see $defs/slotSchemaDSL) normalizes to one of these. Widget↔slot compatibility (which widget may bind which slot kind) and slot↔slot compatibility (structural equality after optional-field widening) are checked against this form by the orchestrator (P1-04.5) — see $defs/slotCompatibilityRule.

values.description: The enum member set; required iff scalar==enum.

oneOf.description: Shorthand for the app-defined item record; see $defs/itemRecord. `list<item>` is the canonical multi-select/checklist slot.

required_fields.description: Names (subset of `fields`) that are required. Fields not listed are optional and may be widened away in a compatible peer schema (SPEC 8.3.4).

itemRecord.description: The app-defined `item` record used by `list<item>` slots (SPEC 8.3.4). `id` is a STABLE, APP-ASSIGNED entry id set on insert — never chosen by a caller on `add` — which makes cross-skill append/patch merges commutative and deterministic. Extra OPTIONAL fields are permitted (schema widening); `id` and `name` are the only required fields, so two skills' `list<item>` slots stay compatible.

slotSchemaDSL.description: The authored STRING form of a slot schema as it appears in a manifest's `shared_state[].schema` (e.g. "list<item>", "scalar<enum(US|Metric)>", "record<{kcal:number, protein_g:number}>"). Shape-checked here; the app normalizes it to $defs/slotType (the authoritative form). Mirrors the widget shorthand→canonical pattern.

acceptsSlotKind.description: Used by per-widget schemas (as a $comment / documented constraint) to declare which slot KIND a widget's `binds_state` accepts — e.g. editable_list → list_item, segmented_toggle → scalar(enum), key_value_panel → record. The concrete slot's kind lives in `shared_state`, so this compatibility is a cross-artifact certification check (P1-04.5), not an in-envelope constraint.

slotCompatibilityRule.description: NON-SCHEMA (documentation node): two skills declaring the same slot name are COMPATIBLE iff their normalized slotType values are structurally equal after optional-field widening — same base `kind`; for scalar, same `scalar` primitive (and, for enum, the same required member set); for list, compatible `of`; for record, the same set of REQUIRED fields (extra OPTIONAL fields on either side are allowed). Incompatible schemas BLOCK the combination with a message naming the differing fields (SPEC 8.3.4). JSON Schema cannot compare two instances, so the orchestrator (P1-04.5) implements this; the rule is recorded here as the single source of truth.
